label: PASS
rationale: After a successful Set to Enable=true, Done=true, DoneOnReset={Power Cycle}, the PowerCycle reset is listed in DoneOnReset, so Done is set false while Enable and DoneOnReset remain as stored; the Get of columns 1-3 reflects that state.

label: FAIL
rationale: DoneOnReset contains Power Cycle and the trajectory performs a successful PowerCycle reset after Done was set true, so Done must be set false; the target Get incorrectly reports Done=true.

label: PASS
rationale: DoneOnReset was successfully set to the empty set, and the source says an empty set means no reset changes Done. After PowerCycle, Done remains true and the Get reflects Enable=true, Done=true, DoneOnReset empty.

label: FAIL
rationale: DoneOnReset was set to the empty set, which shall not change Done on any reset. Since Done was true before PowerCycle, the target Get incorrectly reports Done=false.

label: PASS
rationale: DoneOnReset contains only Power Cycle, not Hardware. A HardwareReset therefore does not automatically clear Done, so the Get of columns 2-3 correctly reports Done=true and the stored DoneOnReset value.

label: FAIL
rationale: Only reset events listed in DoneOnReset set Done false. With DoneOnReset={Power Cycle}, HardwareReset is not listed, so Done should remain true; the target Get incorrectly reports Done=false.

label: PASS
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable was set true, enabling TPER_RESET, and DoneOnReset contains Programmatic. The successful TPerReset therefore sets Done false, and the Get correctly reports Done=false with DoneOnReset still containing PowerCycle and Programmatic.

label: FAIL
rationale: ProgrammaticResetEnable true enables TPER_RESET, and TPER_RESET sets MBRControl.Done false when DoneOnReset contains Programmatic. The target Get incorrectly reports Done=true after a successful TPerReset.
